title: html_decode: 6 chained replace allocs per meta tag
description:
LOCATION: src/card.rs:121-127

FINDING: html_decode() chains 6 .replace() calls. Each call allocates a new String even when there is no match. For HTML with no entities the work is: 6 allocations + 6 full scans of the string, discarding 5 of the resulting Strings. Called for every meta tag value parsed (title, description, image_url, site_name, og:type) — potentially 5-10 times per card fetch.

A single-pass implementation (match on state machine, or using the html-escape crate, or building into a pre-allocated buffer) would reduce this to 1 allocation and 1 scan.

IMPACT: Unnecessary allocations on every card fetch. Minor overhead compared to the HTTP round-trip, but the pattern is an anti-idiom that scales badly if the function is ever called in a tighter context.
